synthesis.summary: BrazilBet partner reported spoofed phishing emails using real player names/emails, asked FT to check for a breach. FT treated as P1 security, made channel private within minutes, ran a live investigation (WAF/edge, DB/ClickHouse, CRM audit, SendGrid) and found no IOCs on FT's side. Partner updated 3x via Intercom; incident.io update posted before moving to Monitoring.

synthesis.summary: Betpipo partner reported repeated logouts from 26 Jun; two earlier fixes (IP-mismatch session invalidation theory, AUTH_IP_BINDING disable) failed to hold and the issue kept recurring. Deep DB investigation 10-13 Jul found the real root cause: a shared UserTokens table where a player's re-auth can invalidate a CRM admin session on UserId collision. 4 fix PRs approved but not yet deployed.

synthesis.summary: H2Bet duplicate bonus grants (BetConstruct 503-after-commit causing retries) confirmed root cause quickly, but 50 days later still ~300 duplicate grants/week with no fix deployed. Intercom ticket closed twice (26 May, 6 Jul by Andrea Azzopardi personally) implying progress with no fix shipped and no ETA ever given to partner.

synthesis.summary: IP-whitelisting change introduced ~6 Apr silently blocked unsubscribe requests for Betiro, Rivalo, Leonbets since 7 Apr - undetected for ~3 months until an unrelated Intercom incident led someone to check. Technical fix merged same day (13 Jul), but no legal/compliance escalation, incomplete impact quantification (Betiro has none 2 days in), and minimizing language used internally. synthesis.summary: Campeon (Lifecycle-driven coin crediting) delayed players' reward coins up to 2h on Sat 11 Jul while notification emails went out promptly, generating a wave of player complaints; reported to FT via Intercom Mon 14 Jul. Engineers traced it same day to a real root cause: bonus 147/199 had auto-claim silently enabled via the Rewards UI on 9 Jul (no audit trail), and the auto-claim credit path serializes under load — 15 consumer pods performed like 1, capping throughput at ~13 msg/s and turning an 82k-message bulk campaign into a 2-hour drain. Partner Manager flagged internally this should be treated as P1 with the partner 'under observation'; incident.io stayed P2 throughout and received no status updates after creation despite the investigation.
key_events: 11 Jul 10:22-12:18 UTC: actual outage — 82k-message bulk reward campaign takes ~1h50m to drain instead of usual 15-30min

synthesis.summary: SBTSolutions (Gergana Ramadanova) reported delayed bonus/activity-trigger processing after player registration on 11 Jul and again on 13 Jul, both traced to Activity Manager queue pileups under high event volume. synthesis.summary: Galabet partner reported players unable to claim reward bonuses despite meeting deposit criteria. Investigation (Carla Santos, then Giancarlo/Matteo) found the underlying LC bonus-code action was configured/added a day after the qualifying player activity, and 340 Rewards bonuses were credited from the action but never claimed — pointing to a partner-side Rewards setup/Operator API issue rather than an FT platform bug. Matteo explained the caveat honestly to the partner (cannot guarantee full fix, may recur via Operator API) and proposed closing 14 Jul; partner still awaiting BetConstruct/Betco feedback. incident.io record itself received only one update since creation and the bot twice questioned inactivity, even though substantive work was ongoing in Intercom.

synthesis.summary: Campeon Nexto users couldn't log in to the CRM due to a v2/v3 URL-version mismatch after login, compounded by browser cache. Root cause found and workaround (clean URL / different browser) delivered within ~15 minutes of the alarm; Patrik personally cleared the Cloudflare cache and restarted crm-api the same day and the fix held. The incident.io record has sat in 'Fixing'/P3 status unclosed for 6+ days since, with no further activity.

10 Jul 14:17 severity raised P4->P3 by Andrea Azzopardi, lead awaiting feedback if still happening
11 Jul 00:33 bot nudge 'no activity, mark resolved?' unanswered
13-14 Jul participants leaving channel, still open
proposals: clean URL / different browser workaround - delivered, effective same day
Cloudflare cache clear + crm-api restart - done 08 Jul
core-xp escalation floated by Giancarlo - not clearly followed up
open_breaches: D15/C10: incident.io stuck in 'Fixing' P3 status 6+ days after the fix was confirmed working same day; bot resolve-nudge from 11 Jul unanswered

synthesis.summary: llm-agent tool execution failing with 'failed to resolve container IP after 3 attempts' on Daytona sandbox cold-resume, affecting meridianbet (and a merged duplicate INC-12203). Root cause confirmed 4 Jul with high confidence: readiness gate probes the control plane while the actual command hits the data plane, which hasn't reconverged on cold resume. Ranked fix filed as 4 follow-ups (real fix = data-plane readiness probe; band-aids = retry/backoff tuning, transient-error copy). No further activity since the RCA; bot nudge asking to resolve 3 days later went unanswered.
key_events: 03 Jul 13:19 alert fires, incident created P3
03 Jul 15:36 duplicate INC-12203 merged in
04 Jul 07:30 RCA posted and self-corrected same message, high confidence, mechanism confirmed
04 Jul 06:52 4 follow-ups filed by Jeremy Curmi
07 Jul 00:21 bot nudge 'no activity, resolve?' — unanswered as of 14 Jul
proposals: probe toolbox data-plane in verifySandboxNetworking (real fix) - filed as follow-up, not confirmed merged/deployed
raise DAYTONA_EXEC_MAX_ATTEMPTS 3->5-6 + widen backoff (band-aid) - filed as follow-up
user-facing transient-error copy - filed as follow-up
reduce cold-resume churn via autostop interval - filed as follow-up
open_breaches: A4: 3rd occurrence of this recurring failure class (after INC-9483, INC-9919) — follow-ups filed but no confirmation any shipped before this went quiet
D14/D15: status stuck 'Investigating' since 4 Jul, bot resolve-nudge unanswered 7+ days, no evidence the real fix was actually deployed

synthesis.summary: 12play/we88 (Momentum) players received activity challenges/rewards 45-50 min late due to RDS overload — a previously known daily 4pm choke pattern (Giancarlo flagged it as recurring on day one) that finally got a real root-cause fix. Multiple engineers (Andrea Envall, Vincent Johansson, Simon Grenefalk et al.) ran a thorough investigation and landed four separate PRs plus two DB instance upgrades over three weeks, materially cutting RDS load. Handling was technically strong throughout, but the incident.io record has been static since 10 Jul and the Incident Lead is now on leave until 10 Aug with the final RabbitMQ/EKS maintenance window still unscheduled.

6-10 Jul: further RDS/RabbitMQ upgrade coordination with Momentum; Incident Lead handed Giancarlo Laferla -> Simon Brown (30 Jun) -> Daniel Lindengren (10 Jul)
14 Jul: Daniel Lindengren departs on leave until 10 Aug, hands remaining RabbitMQ 4.x + EKS maintenance-window scheduling to Andrew Pirotta/Agustín informally — no incident.io lead reassignment

synthesis.summary: SMS distributor service has not processed queued messages for all Coinbar brands since before 30 Apr 2026 — over 1M messages backlogged, critical player notifications never delivered. Root cause (an external SMS provider outage/non-response) identified immediately, but 2.5+ months later there has been zero forward progress: FT has 'repeatedly requested status updates from the SMS provider' with no concrete feedback, and the channel is now purely automated (daily recurring per-brand alerts auto-merged in) with the Incident Lead rotated 8+ times among SOC/SRE engineers with no substantive handoff notes.
key_events: 30 Apr: incident created, 1M+ message backlog across Coinbar brands, provider unresponsive since 'end of April'
25-29 May: Andrea Azzopardi repeatedly asks lead (Matteo Rapisarda) for an explanation of why the incident is open and what SOC should do — no answer for 4 days
29 May: Matteo finally explains — 'not much to do from SOC', pulls in Gustav Tjäder to determine 'where they stand' and 'next course of action'

29 May onward: zero further human commentary in channel; only auto-merges of recurring per-brand SMS alerts and repeated Incident Lead handoffs (Nazareno -> Joachim -> James -> Matteo -> Simon -> James -> ...)
14 Jul: Incident Lead reassigned again to James Hodgson, still no substantive update since 29 May
proposals: Escalate to SMS provider for status — done repeatedly, no concrete response ever received
Gustav Tjäder asked to advise next course of action (29 May) — no reply ever seen in channel
open_breaches: A4: chronic multi-month issue never escalated to a decisive fix or vendor-management track, just re-merged and re-monitored daily
D12/D15: the one substantive question raised (29 May, to Gustav) was never answered or tracked — record shows 6+ weeks of pure automated churn with no owner actually progressing it

synthesis.summary: 12Play (onetwoplay) partner reported a Promotion Calendar's Chinese comms profile mysteriously missing from the FT CRM backoffice, though the calendar itself remained live and visible to players (no end-user impact). Rewards team (Daniel Lindengren) found the root cause 13-14 Jul: a backoffice display bug affecting orphan-but-live door variations, not data loss. Partner was informed with a concrete fix ETA; two PRs opened 14 Jul and handed off to Sam Lundborg for testing 15 Jul.

synthesis.summary: Recurring H2Bet nightly ClickHouse-writer queue pileup (RabbitMQ backlog + a payload type-mismatch bug on the Status field), consolidated into this 'DO NOT CLOSE' bucket incident since 26 Jun. Genuine root-cause work happened (a separate ClickHouse log-volume-full config bug was found and fixed via PR #435, merged 9 Jul; queue restarted/rolled 9 Jul), but the actual producer/consumer schema mismatch causing consumer errors has never been fixed, and 2 more matching alerts were merged in as recently as 12 Jul. A planned 'close Monday 13 Jul if stable' was never actioned — incident.io has had no update since 9 Jul, and the last human message (14 Jul) is a vague 'need to sanity check on this one, then ask them what they're doing'.
key_events: 26 Jun 03:15 UTC — grouped under this bucket incident (absorbing INC-10603 + INC-10901), 28 alerts attached
27 Jun — pileup recurs overnight, RDS scaled 4xLarge->8xLarge, clickhouse-writer scaled to 90 pods, Status-field unmarshal error confirmed
9 Jul — separate ClickHouse log-volume-full bug found and fixed (PR #435 merged); CH pod restarted, plan to monitor over weekend and close Monday 13 Jul
12 Jul — two more matching alerts (INC-13508, INC-13463) merged in, incident still open past the promised close date
14 Jul 15:29 CEST — last human message: 'need to sanity check on this one, then ask them what they're doing'; no incident.io update since 9 Jul
proposals: Fix Status field schema/type mismatch end-to-end (producer vs consumer struct) — proposed repeatedly since 26 Jun, still not implemented as of 14 Jul
ClickHouse log-volume/rotation config fix — implemented, PR #435 merged 9 Jul
Define sustainable autoscaling/throughput plan for clickhouse-writer at nightly peak — proposed 30 Jun/1 Jul, no evidence of implementation
Restart/reboot ClickHouse + roll clickhouse-writer pods, monitor and close if stable — done 9 Jul, planned close Monday 13 Jul — not actioned
open_breaches: A4: root-cause payload type-mismatch bug (Status field) identified 26 Jun, still unfixed 19 days later; two more instances merged in as late as 12 Jul
D15: incident.io stale — no update since 9 Jul despite the promised 13 Jul close date passing and further recurrence
